summary: Excellent soundboard‑plus‑audience matrix capture of a stellar 1976 show, highlighted by a soaring Eyes → Music Never Stopped sequence and a superb Other One/Comes A Time stretch.
review: The recording is primarily a clean Charlie Miller SBD that’s been patched with audience (AUD) material where the board drops out – still very listenable and praised as “very good” despite occasional patch roughness. Musically this night shines, especially the second set: Eyes into Music Never Stopped, The Other One flowing into an emotional Comes A Time, plus standout moments from Samson & Deliah, He’s Gone, Franklin’s Tower and a fiery Sugaree.

ai_rating.rationale: Over a dozen reviews rate the show 4‑5 stars, consistently praising the second‑set performance while noting only minor AUD patch issues; the blend of strong musical content and good technical quality yields a high overall score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Primary Charlie Miller soundboard source, with audience (AUD) patches for missing sections. Aud patches are described as “less appealing” but not enough to spoil the listening experience; overall crisp and balanced.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is solid but uneven, with a few average cuts; the second set is a seamless, high‑energy journey where songs flow into one another (Eyes → Music Never Stopped, The Other One → Comes A Time, etc.), delivering the show’s emotional peak.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals described as excellent throughout; his guitar work and trading licks with Keith are highlighted.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass work praised, especially on Eyes (“throwing down”) and leading the transition into Music Never Stopped.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s keyboard contributions noted as “amazing” and he trades licks with the drummers; overall keyboards praised.
band_member_comments.Drums: Solid drum foundation with a brief drum break mentioned; no major complaints.
